Attorney, Scott D. Righthand of the law office of Scott Righthand (415-544-0115) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Karyn N. Chandler of the law offices of Lewis Brisbois (415-438-6639) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to punitive damages allegations in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Kiel Burnner is GRANTED without leave to amend. The Second Amended Complaint fails to allege sufficient ultimate facts to show despicable conduct by Lyft nor does it appear that Mr. Brunner is able to do so at this time. If Mr. Brunner believes in good faith that he has learned additional facts through discovery or factual investigation that supports imposition of punitive damages against Lyft, he may seek leave to amend. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-24-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brittany Rogers, Esq. (415-544-0115) of Law Office of Scott Righthand for Plaintiff/Opposing party Kiel Brunner.
Karyn Chandler, Esq, (415-438-6639) for Defendant/Moving party LYFT, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT LYFT, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Defendant Lyft's motion to strike punitive damages in plaintiff Kiel Brunner's first amended complaint is granted and Mr. Brunner is granted twenty days leave to amend. The notice of motion complies with CRC 2.1322. Mr. Brunner has not adequately alleged a claim for punitive damages. The first amended complaint contains boilerplate allegations that Lyft acted with "oppression, fraud, malice, and a willful, wanton, conscious and/or reckless disregard for the safety of others." Any theory that Lyft knew upon hiring Mr. Vegas that he would commit illegal and unsafe acts is not supported by ultimate facts explaining the scope and extent of Lyft's knowledge. Because it appears reasonably possible amendment would cure the deficiency, Mr. Brunner is granted twenty days leave to amend to adequately allege a claim of punitive damages if he can do so in good faith. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Order is signed. Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR #11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/CPK)
